repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
PR #30843 introduced a regression in the default titlestring: if the containing directory was the PWD, it would render it as "."

## Solution
Add `:p` to the titlestring.


~~I've validated these tests pass if you use the old C-based implementation by running~~

I've validated the new tests pass if you use the old C-based implementation by running the below. Obviously, the modified tests fail :)

```
git checkout HEAD~1 -- src/nvim/buffer.c
git show cedf155 -- src/nvim/buffer.c |git apply -R
make
make functionaltest TEST_FILE=test/functional/ui/title_spec.lua
```

Fixes https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/32411

comment_data.body:
is there a reason to use escaped `\"` instead of just `'` ? 
```suggestion
	    set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{empty(expand('%'))\ ?\ ''\ :\ expand('%:p:~:h')}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6951,7 +6949,7 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	error will be given.
 
 	The default (empty) behaviour is equivalent to: >vim
-	    set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{expand(\"%:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim
+	    set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{empty(expand(\"%\"))\ ?\ \"\"\ :\ expand(\"%:p:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim

comment_data.body: `cd /` should work just fine on Windows
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,6 +37,26 @@ describe('title', function()
     end)
   end)
 
+  it('has correct default title with named file in PWD', function()
+    local expected = (is_os('win') and 'myfile (C:\\) - Nvim' or 'myfile (/) - Nvim')
+    command('set title')
+    command(is_os('win') and 'cd C:\\' or 'cd /')

comment_data.body: why do we care about the `empty(expand('%'))` check? if the directory is "useful" for a file, why isn't it useful for the `[No name]` case?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -9686,7 +9685,7 @@ local options = {
         error will be given.
 
         The default (empty) behaviour is equivalent to: >vim
-            set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{expand(\"%:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim
+            set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{empty(expand(\"%\"))\ ?\ \"\"\ :\ expand(\"%:p:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim

comment_data.body: these test descriptions are hard to understand. also "has correct" is never needed (i know the existing tests have it, but still), all tests assume correct behavior.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -37,6 +37,26 @@ describe('title', function()
     end)
   end)
 
+  it('has correct default title with named file in PWD', function()
+    local expected = (is_os('win') and 'myfile (C:\\) - Nvim' or 'myfile (/) - Nvim')
+    command('set title')
+    command(is_os('win') and 'cd C:\\' or 'cd /')
+    command('file myfile')
+    screen:expect(function()
+      eq(expected, screen.title)
+    end)
+  end)
+
+  it('has correct default title with absolute path in PWD', function()

comment_data.body: This was an attempt to match the behavior of the C code before. However, I agree that having the PWD could be useful, so I don't mind adding that in!

         error will be given.
 
         The default (empty) behaviour is equivalent to: >vim
-            set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{expand(\"%:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim
+            set titlestring=%t%(\ %M%)%(\ \(%{empty(expand(\"%\"))\ ?\ \"\"\ :\ expand(\"%:p:~:h\")}\)%)%a\ -\ Nvim

comment_data.body: Nah, was just following the existing convention.

comment_data.body: I've gone ahead and resolved this. It required modifying the existing tests, as now we've made the directory persistent, but I think that's fine :) though maybe it upgrades this PR from a `fix`?

comment_regular_data.body:
Thanks, will be nice to close that issue. Can we drop the "help" handling though? That's not important, and seems to really add noise to the title expression.

> I've validated these tests pass if you use the old C-based implementation by running

Nice! Really adds confidence.
@justinmk Fair enough! I've removed the `help` text :)
